thread: Who is the newer grain seller, and does he matter?

evidence.clue: Miri remembers adults saying there was a newer grain man on the road before Millhaven collapsed.
The village ledger and witness pattern repeat Millhaven's weaker clue: a new supplier arrived at summer's end with slightly better prices and a forgettable manner.
Sylvanas revisits the old grain seller in memory and realizes he came north out of the same feeder-road web they are now tracing south toward Barov-linked depots.

A second grain-road contact on the Andorhal approach uses the exact same farewell — Safe roads. What's left of them. — confirming the phrase as recurring network language even though the man is not identified as the same person.

readerKnows: The grain-seller clue has become a pattern rather than a lone strange memory. The repeated farewell ties multiple road contacts to the same social signal.
povCharacterKnows: Sylvanas knows the phrase matters. Jaina hears the repetition too. Neither woman can yet prove whether the two men are linked by identity, role, or merely shared network speech.
notes: Keep the thread unresolved. Chapter6 confirms recurring signal, not solved infiltrator.
